articles.title: Former Israeli minister accuses Netanyahu of humiliating Israel
articles.summary: Former Israeli minister accuses Netanyahu of humiliating Israel Former Israeli defence minister Avigdor Lieberman accused Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of turning Israel into a “banana republic” amid growing criticism of US-led efforts to reach an agreement with Iran. Speaking to Channel 12’s Meet the Press, Lieberman argued that any deal leaving Iran’s leadership in power would be a “catastrophe” for Israel. Lieberman also criticised reported provisions of a potential agreement, including the possible release of frozen Iranian assets and the resumption of unrestricted Iranian oil and gas

articles.title: Ex-Israeli security chief says 'Iran won the war'
articles.summary: Ex-Israeli security chief says 'Iran won the war' Former head of Israel's domestic security council, Giora Eiland, said Iran emerged as the winner of the latest conflict involving the United States and Israel, arguing that both Washington and Tel Aviv now face a difficult strategic situation. Speaking to Israel’s Channel 12, the Israeli security official  said, “Iran won the war, perhaps by a few points, but it is a clear victory.” Eiland said Iran’s leadership remained intact and had demonstrated resilience throughout the conflict, allowing Tehran to project strength in its dealings with

articles.title: CNN: White House shooting suspect dies of injuries
articles.summary: CNN: White House shooting suspect dies of injuries The suspect involved in an armed incident near the White House has died from injuries sustained during the confrontation, according to CNN, citing the US Secret Service. The incident prompted a major security response on Saturday evening, with police and federal agents deploying around the White House complex. US President Donald Trump was inside the White House at the time, according to earlier reports. The area was cordoned off and access restricted as authorities responded. Kash Patel said FBI personnel were assisting the US Secret Service

articles.title: Iran reviews negotiations with Iraq, Turkey and Qatar
articles.summary: Iran reviews negotiations with Iraq, Turkey and Qatar Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi held separate phone calls with his counterparts from Iraq, Turkey and Qatar to discuss regional developments and ongoing Pakistan-mediated talks between Tehran and Washington. The discussions included Iraqi foreign minister Fuad Hussein, Turkish foreign minister Hakan Fidan and Qatari Prime Minister and foreign minister Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani. articles.title: Egyptian President urges diplomacy in call with Trump and regional leaders
articles.summary: Egyptian President urges diplomacy in call with Trump and regional leaders Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi called for a diplomatic agreement to end current tensions with Iran and prevent renewed escalation during a phone call with US President Donald Trump and several Middle Eastern leaders, according to a statement from Egypt’s presidency. The call included Abdullah II, Mohammed bin Salman, Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Recep Tayyip Erdogan and Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani. articles.title: Iran sees chance for new regional chapter, ambassador says
articles.summary: Iran sees chance for new regional chapter, ambassador says Iran’s ambassador to Saudi Arabia, Alireza Enayati, said regional conditions now provide an opportunity for countries to work together on rebuilding and development. In a post on X, Enayati called for nations across the region to unite and cooperate in reconstruction efforts through mutual assistance. Enayati described Iran as a country that remains resilient during difficult times and endures through history. articles.title: Former CIA chief attacks proposed Iran agreement
articles.summary: Former CIA chief attacks proposed Iran agreement Former CIA director and Secretary of State Mike Pompeo criticised the emerging agreement between the United States and Iran, arguing that it resembled the approach taken by officials involved in the Obama-era nuclear deal. Pompeo said the reported agreement appeared to come from the “Wendy Sherman–Robert Malley–Ben Rhodes playbook”, referring to key figures associated with the original Iran nuclear accord that President Donald Trump later abandoned. articles.title: Iran official cites Persia’s defeat of Rome to frame standoff with US
articles.summary: Iran official cites Persia’s defeat of Rome to frame standoff with US Iranian foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei invoked the Roman emperor Marcus Julius Philippus in a pointed message directed at Washington, saying that when Rome marched east against Persia, it was ultimately forced to make peace on Sasanian terms. Baghaei, who holds a doctorate from Tehran University, has frequently referenced historical and literary figures in his public comments. articles.title: IRGC dismisses Trump comments on Iran nuclear talks
articles.summary: IRGC dismisses Trump comments on Iran nuclear talks Iran’s Revolutionary Guard dismissed US President Donald Trump’s latest social media comments as propaganda, saying Tehran has made no commitments regarding its nuclear programme. According to Iran’s Fars news agency, the IRGC said the nuclear issue has not been discussed at the current stage of negotiations despite Trump previously describing it as a central condition of any agreement. articles.title: Police, FBI swarm White House after reports of shots fired
articles.summary: Police, FBI swarm White House after reports of shots fired Police and security forces have descended on the area surrounding the White House on Saturday evening following reports of shots fired, according to AFP journalists. US President Donald Trump was in the White House at the time working to negotiate a deal with Iran. Access to the area has been cordoned off and National Guard troops have blocked an AFP reporter from entering. FBI Director Kash Patel said that the FBI are on the scene supporting the US secret services responding to the incident.

articles.title: Gaza flotilla activists return to Ireland, UK
articles.summary: Gaza flotilla activists return to Ireland, UK British and Irish activists onboard the Gaza-bound flotilla returned to London and Dublin on Saturday after being abducted and imprisoned by Israeli forces. Among the Irish activists was Margaret Connolly, sister of Irish President Catherine Connolly, who recounted being held with other detainees by Israel in “containers”, where several people risked hypothermia. articles.title: Iran, Pakistan send revised peace proposal to US, sources tell Reuters
articles.summary: Iran, Pakistan send revised peace proposal to US, sources tell Reuters Iran and Pakistan have submitted a revised proposal to the United States to bring about the end of the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Reuters reported, citing two Pakistani sources familiar with the negotiations.  The US response to the proposal is expected by Sunday, the sources added, confirming US President Donald Trump’s earlier comments to Axios that he will make the decision on whether to resume the war or make a deal by then.

articles.title: The Surprising Joys of a Crowded Hiking Trail
articles.summary: Many international landmarks have been designed to attract and accommodate enormous numbers of tourists. That’s not the case when it comes to natural scenery. Outdoors types often love to hike for miles into the wilderness precisely to get away from the crowds; those people shudder at the very idea of a 13-mile hike where you’re constantly close to other walkers. Bloomberg News Ideas & Culture Senior Writer Felix Salmon joins Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the surprising joys of a crowded hiking trail. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Will the Dollar Remain King in a Cashless Society?
articles.summary: The dollar has been the world's reserve currency for decades, but it's history goes back centuries – to before the United States was the United States. Brendan Greeley, author of the new book "The Almighty Dollar: 500 Years of the World's Most Powerful Money," joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the currency's history, explain its dominance in global finance, and look ahead to the role it'll play in a world in which cash is no longer king. (Source: Bloomberg)
